content.full_text:
Re:
Fern Polrrrcel Pnecrrcps CoMMrssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 e Sacramento. CA g5gl4_232g
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
Aprrl22,2O08
Carol Jimenez
Deputy Attomey General
Consumer Law Section
Office of the Attorney General
300 Spring Street
Los Angeles, California 90013
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-08-056
Dear Ms. Jimenez:
This letter responds to your reqllest for advice regarding the disclosure provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").' Thir letter is basJd solely on the facts presented; the
Fair Political Practices Commission (the ..Commission") does not act as the finder of f.act
when it renders advice. (rn re oglesbv (r975) r Fppc ops. 71.)
QUESTTON
- 
what are your reporting requirements for a payment received from your ex-husband
for accompanying your daughter on a trip to visit a college campLls where your daughter may
be enrolling in the Fall?
CONCLUSION
The payment need not be reported as it is neither a gift nor income under the Act.
FACTS
You are a deputy attomey general in the consllmer law section of the Los Angeles
gffit: In that capacity' you are involved in consumer protection activities solely related to
health care issues. You are designated as a category 2 Lmployee in your og.n"i,, conflict-
' The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 8l0(x) through g l0l4. All
statutory ret'erenses are ttl the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
Prrlitical Practices Commission are contained in Sections l8l l0 through lgggT of Title 2 0f the Calitirrnia c0de
of Regulations. All regulakrry ref'elences are k) Title 2. Division o orihe Califirrnia Code of Regulations,
unless otherwise indieated.


File No. A-08-056
Page No. 2
of-interest code.2 Designation in that category provides that you are required to disclose cil
financial interests, except real property troiaings.
Your daughter-is a senior in high school and is currently applying to various colleges
with the intent of enrolling next semester. She has been accepted at the University of
Colorado, among others. She would like to visit certain colleges that have offered her
admission before deciding where she will attend.
Your ex-husband is an attomey in private practice engaged in business litigation and
mainly represents small businesses. He is not invtlved in anyttring that may relate to your
job activities as a deputy attomey general. However, because of y-our broad disclosure
requirements, he falls into the class of persons (everyone) you must disclose under the
enumerated financial interests.
- 
You and your ex-husband would like to assist your daughter in her visits to evaluate
the various colleges in order to facilitate her decision as to where she would like to enroll.
However, because of her-a€e, one of you must accompany her on these visits. your husband
indicated that if you would go with her (so that he doesn'ihave to) he would pay rhe
expenses.
In March of this year' yotl accompanied your daughter on a trip to the University of
Colorado in Boulder. The cost of the trip was $iSO fo. yiur roundtrip airfare, $150 per night
for a three night hotel stay where you and your daughteishared a room, and g60 for a rentar
car. Your husband paid for the trip.
ANALYSIS
one of the stated purposes of the Act it to ensure that public officials ,.perform their
duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests or the
financial interests of persons who have supported them." (Section gl00l(b).) To accomplish
this purpose, the Act requires agencies to divelop a conflict-of-interest code that enumerates
the positions of those public officials who are involved in the making or participating in the
making of govemmental decisions and requires them to disclose certain financial interests
!1{ Tuy foreseeably be materially affected by any governmental decision. (Sections g7300,
87302.)
You hold a position that your agency has determined is involved in such decisions.
Accordingly, you position has been designated in its conflict-of-interest code and, as a
category 2 employee, you are required to report any income or gifts (above the statutory
thresholds) received from any person.
The Act requires.every agency to adopt a conflict-of-interest code that enumerates all positions within
the agency with duties that involve the making or participation in the making of decisions that may foreseeably
have a material effect on any financial interest. tSiction g7302.) As a designated employee, your agency has
made that determination with respect to your position.


File No. A-08-056
Page No. 3
The Acr provides the following definitions:
"Payment" is defined under Section g2044 as:
"Payment" means a payment, distribution, transfer, loan, advance,
deposit, gift or other rendering of money, property, services or
anything erse of value, whether tangible oi iriongibr".',
A "gift" is defined under Section g202g as:
"any payment that confers a personal benefit on the recipient, to the
extent that consideration of equal or greater value is not received and
includes a rebate or discount in the piice of anything of value unless
the rebate or discount is made in the regular course of business to
members of the public without regard to official status.,,
"Income" is defined under Section g2030 as:
"(a) . . . a payment received, including but not limited to any salary,
wage, advance, dividend, interest, rent, proceeds from any sare, gilt,
including any gift of food or beverage, rour, forgiveness o. puy*"nt or
indebtedness received by the filer, riimbrr..*.rt for expenser, p".
diem, or contribution to an insurance or pension p.ogru', paid by any
person other than an employer, and incruding any community p.op.rty
interest in the income of a spouse.,,
. 
Becallse your husband provided the payment in exchange for your travel and
time to accompany your daughter, and in oroei that he would rrit nur. to perrorm ttrat
function, you provided consideration of equal or greater value in exchangl for the
payment. Accordingly, the payment was not a giit.
Absent an ex-cePtion, the payment would therefore be considered income.
However, Section 82030 (b) lists certain exceptions to the definition or ir.o-",
among which are "child supporr payments." ('section g2030(b)(7).)
Nothing in the Act requires that the child support payments be court ordered.3
Black's Law Dictionary defines child support as ....-. monlypaid by one parent to
another toward the expenses of children 
-of 
the marriage.', fu" .on iina no logical
reason to conclude that, under the facts presented, o piy*"nt by a father to a"mother
to take their daughter on a trip to view iuniversity .urnpr., in order to determine if
the child wishes to attend colrege there, is not o poyrn.ni to sllpport ..the expenses,, of
the child.
r Nor does it provide a definition for child support.


File No. A-08-056
Page No. 4
Accordingly, any payment received from you ex-husband that is used towards
taking you daughter to visit a college campus for ihe pllrposes of determining if she
wants to attend' including payment for your airfare, rt oria hotel room, rentalcar, and
any other payments reasonably related to that purpose are not income and need not be
reported.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (g16) 322-5660
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
By: William
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
WJL:jgl

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: - 
what are your reporting requirements for a payment received from your ex-husband
for accompanying your daughter on a trip to visit a college campLls where your daughter may
be enrolling in the Fall?

CONCLUSION: The payment need not be reported as it is neither a gift nor income under the Act.
